summary: Colombian artist known for exaggerated, rotund figures. Most commonly identified by Abu Ghraib series (guards/dogs torturing prisoners), Death of Pablo Escobar, Mona Lisa Age 12, and Presidential Family. Dove of Peace sculpture replaced a sculpture destroyed by a bomb. Studied at the Prado copying Goya.

works.name: Abu Ghraib series (2005)
works.description: Over 80 paintings of guards and dogs torturing blindfolded, nude prisoners. Inspired by Seymour Hersh article in The New Yorker. Donated to Berkeley Museum of Art. Botero requested they be exhibited at all times as a 'permanent accusation.' Wikipedia

works.name: Mona Lisa, Age Twelve
works.description: Parody of the Mona Lisa depicting the subject as a bloated, childlike figure with a face filling a quarter of the canvas. Created prior to the 1963 US tour of the original Mona Lisa. Hangs in the Botero Museum in Bogota's Candelaria neighborhood.

works.name: The Presidential Family

comprehensive_summary:
Fernando Botero was a Colombian artist known for his distinctive exaggerated, rotund figures, which he said emphasized 'volume' and 'sensuality of form.' He credited the development of his style to an early still life of a mandolin with an extremely small central sound hole. He studied at the Prado in Spain, copying Goya paintings, and was expelled from Jesuit high school for an article praising Picasso's Cubism.

His Abu Ghraib series (2005) contains over 80 paintings responding to the torture scandal, showing guards and dogs violently humiliating blindfolded prisoners, inspired by a Seymour Hersh article in The New Yorker. He donated the paintings to the Berkeley Museum of Art and requested they be exhibited at all times as a 'permanent accusation.' The Death of Pablo Escobar shows a giant man with closed eyes holding a gun up to the sky, riddled with bullets as he stands on a rooftop.

Mona Lisa, Age Twelve is a parody of Leonardo's original with a bloated childlike face filling a quarter of the canvas, created prior to a 1963 US tour of the real Mona Lisa; it hangs in the Botero Museum in Bogota's La Candelaria neighborhood. The Presidential Family alludes to Las Meninas (with the artist peeking from behind an easel) and is a riff on Goya's The Family of Carlos IV. He also parodied Raphael's Portrait of Pope Leo X and created a double self-portrait based on Velazquez.

Botero adapted his signature style to sculpture in the 1980s (Maternity, Roman Soldier) and created the Dove of Peace sculpture from the remains of his Bird sculpture after it was destroyed in a terrorist bombing. Dozens of his large bronze sculptures are displayed in Botero Plaza in Medellin, and the Broadgate Venus (a five-ton bronze nude) stands in front of London's Liverpool Street Station. His Violence series depicts car bombings and kidnappings in Colombia. He died in September 2023, eulogized by Colombian president Gustavo Petro.
